Let q = 𝑣(1 − 𝑎) + (1 − 𝑣)(1 − 𝑟𝑑 − 𝑟𝑒 ). After the first round of credit creation, the total new loans for shadow
banks and commercial banks is Bq . From q = 𝑣(1 − 𝑎) + (1 − 𝑣)(1 − 𝑟𝑑 − 𝑟𝑒 ) ， we can get q = 1 −
[(1 − 𝑣)(𝑟𝑑 + 𝑟𝑒 ) + 𝑣𝑎]. From [(1 − 𝑣)(𝑟𝑑 + 𝑟𝑒 ) + 𝑣𝑎]＜1, we can get 0 < q < 1.

Therefore, if the original deposit is B, after n-time deposit derivation from the entire banking system, the final
money supply is as follow：
